name: Topological Sort (Kahn's Algorithm)
description: Topological sorting linearly orders the vertices of a directed acyclic graph so every edge points forward: Kahn's algorithm computes in-degrees, repeatedly removes a zero in-degree vertex into the order while decrementing its neighbors, and enqueues newly freed vertices; if fewer than all vertices are output, the graph contained a cycle.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Directed acyclic graph"]
    N2["Compute in-degrees"]
    N3["Enqueue zero in-degree"]
    N4["Dequeue node u"]
    N5["Append u to order"]
    N6["Reduce neighbor in-deg"]
    N7{"Any neighbor now zero?"}
    N8["Enqueue those neighbors"]
    N9{"Queue empty?"}
    N10{"Output == all nodes?"}
    N11["Topological order / cycle"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N7 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|no| N4
    N9 -->|yes| N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Introduction to Algorithms (4th ed.)
sources.authors: Cormen, T. H.; Leiserson, C. E.; Rivest, R. L.; Stein, C.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
